carbon_policy_signal.item: EU renewable permitting liberalisation stalled by far-right–green coalition; delays extend gas-dependency and lift ETS compliance demand through 2026–27.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Slower renewable build extends thermal generation and gas dispatch; higher gas burn and coal avoidance increase EU power-sector ETS liability, supporting carbon price floor.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (145 days old, as of 2025-12-26) and clean-dark spread stale (140 days old, as of 2025-12-31). Fuel-switch analysis and coal merit-order signals cannot be verified without current refresh.
risk_flags: EU storage at 36.7% (14th-percentile, 13.7pp below seasonal) — refill pace into summer will determine autumn/winter margin adequacy; TTF +28.6% monthly already reflects this tension.

GB power at 122.71 EUR/MWh (90th-percentile) vs DE power at 135.39 EUR/MWh (75th-percentile) — GB premium signals structural tightness; daily jump of +10.7% yesterday warrants watch for nuclear/wind supply disruption.

Coal data stale (145 days old at 96 USD/t, 8th-percentile) — cannot verify whether historical low has held or normalised; clean-dark spread also stale (140 days); fuel-switch signals unreliable until refresh.

US sanctions waiver extension and record US industrial gas demand into 2026–27 reduce NW Europe LNG import arbitrage; TTF +3.12% daily and +9.93% weekly suggest market already pricing in tighter LNG supply.

base.summary: Storage deficit and LNG tightness persist; thermal margin underpins 130–140 EUR/MWh DE power; TTF 50–55 EUR/MWh.

downside.summary: EU–US trade deal reduces euro and industrial demand; renewables permitting surprise acceleration cuts gas call; mild weather slows storage draw.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or Suez disruption tightens crude and LNG spot pricing; US sanctions waiver expires; record US gas demand accelerates NW Europe LNG squeeze.

themes: Structural storage deficit and tight LNG arb into summer
GB premium widening as thermal baseload call rises
Geopolitical crude/supply tightness feeding fuel-switch economics
Renewable permitting delays extend gas dependency through 2026–27
top_drivers: EU storage deficit (13.7pp below seasonal, 36.7% full) forcing earlier thermal call and tightening summer refill timeline; TTF +28.6% monthly reflects this.
GB power premium (90th-percentile, +10.7% daily) signals acute baseload tightness; crude/LNG tightness from US sanctions waiver extension and record US gas demand supports fuel-switch margin.
Renewable permitting delays (far-right/green bloc coalition slowing EU liberalisation) extend gas-dependency into 2026–27 and lift structural TTF floor.
top_takeaway: GB power at 90th-percentile, EU storage 13.7pp below seasonal norm, and crude/LNG tightness from US sanctions extension and record US gas demand support thermal generation; coal historically cheap but stale data limits conviction.

watchlist.metric: US Russian oil sanctions policy & Strait of Hormuz status
watchlist.why: Crude supply closure (Hormuz + extended waiver uncertainty) supports oil prices, drives fuel-switching away from gas and supports thermal generation merit in EU power stack.

watchlist.metric: EU renewable permitting liberalisation (far-right/green bloc slowdown)
watchlist.why: Delays extend structural gas dependency into 2026–27; slower capacity build increases baseload reliance and upward pressure on TTF through medium term.

watchlist.metric: EU–US trade deal finalisation & Trump auto tariff decision
watchlist.why: Euro weakness and industrial growth headwinds from trade friction could suppress Q3–Q4 EU power/gas demand and ease storage refill pressure.

watchlist.metric: Italy's energy spending carveout outcome in EU fiscal rules
watchlist.why: If approved, Italian energy subsidies could influence regional power/gas pricing and signalling on EU-wide energy cost mitigation.
Historical 8th-percentile low at 96 USD/t needs current confirmation; fuel-switch analysis hinges on verifying whether coal margin has shifted or normalised.

watchlist_dated.name: US sanctions policy or Hormuz closure update
watchlist_dated.why: Crude supply signal cascades into LNG arb and EU fuel-switch economics within 24–48h.

watchlist_dated.name: EU trade deal finalization vote
watchlist_dated.why: EUR weakness / growth signal transmits to industrial demand and power/gas curves across summer.
